name: Harmonize mixed-unit lab data to US conventional units
prompt: Read lab_data.csv with patient lab results containing mixed units and formatting issues. Harmonize all values to US conventional units (Serum_Creatinine in mg/dL, Glucose in mg/dL, Hemoglobin in g/dL). Steps: (1) parse numeric formats (handle scientific notation, European decimals with comma, whitespace), (2) for each value check if it's within the expected range for that analyte — Serum_Creatinine range 0.2-20.0 mg/dL, Glucose range 30-500 mg/dL, Hemoglobin range 3-20 g/dL — if outside range, apply the appropriate conversion factor (Serum_Creatinine: multiply by 0.0113 to convert from µmol/L; Glucose: divide by 0.0555 to convert from mmol/L; Hemoglobin: multiply by 0.1 to convert from g/L), (3) format all values to 2 decimal places. Write harmonized_labs.csv with the same columns but all values standardized.

criteria.description: All Serum_Creatinine values in 0.2-20.0 mg/dL range
All Glucose values in 30-500 mg/dL range
All Hemoglobin values in 3-20 g/dL range
Spot-check conversions for P001 and P011
